summary: CPU-only inference latency across the kubelm tier ladder
rationale: kubelm is a CPU-only model family spanning a resource spectrum (smallest local box -> dev machine); per-tier CPU latency is a first-class metric (PROJECT.md commitment #7, <5s/step target). Prior benches ran GPU-offloaded; this is the first CPU-only characterization.

host.note: M1 Max has ~400GB/s unified memory bandwidth + Accelerate BLAS; an UPPER bound. Commodity/constrained CPUs (the real ultra-edge target) will be several x slower. Relative ordering across tiers holds.

tool: llama-bench -ngl 0 -p 2048 -n 128 -r 3 (CPU-only; validated vs -ngl 99: 2B tg 48.6 CPU vs 124.5 Metal, 2.6x => -ngl 0 is genuine CPU)

real_scenario_runs.note: Full K8sGPT-MCP investigation (not synthetic), llama-server -ngl 0, model latency only (excludes tool/cluster time).
real_scenario_runs.outcome: complete, rubric PASSED, schema 3/3, refcalls passed (correct conclusion)

findings: 0.8B is decisively the fastest CPU tier (~1.6x the 2B's prompt-processing, best token-gen) and the only tier close to the <5s/step target -> validates the ultra-edge rung.

Architectural: Qwen3.5-2B processes prompts FASTER than Qwen2.5-1.5B despite more params (hybrid linear-attention is cheaper on large tool-schema prompts). The ladder is not strictly monotonic in params for prompt-processing.
No tier achieves <5s on a COLD step even on M1 Max; on weaker CPUs the gap widens -> the latency commitment is real pressure and the ultra-edge tier's reason to exist.

Real-scenario proof: 0.8B on 2 cores completes a correct K8sGPT investigation in ~76s (5 steps, ~15s/step) vs 2B on 6 cores at ~191s (4 steps, ~48s/step). Per-step cost is dominated by prompt processing (heaviest step = 52s on a 3104-token prompt), so latency scales with accumulated tool-output size, not step count.
FOLLOW-UP: define the optimal/acceptable CPU+RAM envelope per tier (sweep cores x model x prompt size) so sizing guidance maps a config -> expected per-step latency, not just RAM floor.
